title: An ir emitter for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, methods, systems, devices and modules thereof
abstract: The present invention discloses mean and methods for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, comprising a device or a garment comprising a device configured for administering patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, comprising emitting by an emitter electromagnetic radiation ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, e.g., the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1: 1: 1 to 1:200:8000; providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius, optionally with a continuous or pulsed cooling; when emitting, the emitter is pushed towards the patient, and presses the outermost abdomen surface inwardly towards the intestines for at least 4 mm; said pressure is provided continuously or in an intermittent manner for a period of time of 10 to 60 min.
claims:
Claims 1. An IR emitting device for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease by administering patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, comprising an emitter for emitting electromagnetic radiation ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius; when emitting, said emitter is pushed towards the patient, so that said outermost abdomen surface is pressed inwards towards the intestines for one the following: at least 4 mm or about 50 mm, at least 4 mm; or otherwise at least 20 mm, at least 40 mm , no more than about 95 mm, no more than about 85 mm. 2. The device of claim 1 , further comprising a belly massager whilst emitting IR for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease by administering patient's outermost abdomen surface; characterized by that it further comprises a massaging module provided with means for applying said emitter towards patient body with forces selected from high forces (1.5 to 10 kg cm' 2 ) and low forces (0.1-1.5 kg cm' 2 ). 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1: 1:1 to 1:20:200; 4. The device of claim 1, wherein the electro radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1:20:200 to 1:200:8000. 5. The device of claim 1, provided in a wearable garment. 6. A wearable garment comprising the device of claim 1. 7. The device of claim 2, provided in a wearable garment. 8. A wearable garment comprising device of claim 2. 9. The device of claim 1 , comprising a robot-arm for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease by administering the patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission. 10. The device of claim 2, comprising a robot-arm for treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease by administering patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission. 11. A static system comprising the device of claim 1. 12. A static system comprising the device of claim 2. 22 SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26) 13. A method of treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, comprising administering the patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, emitted by an emitter, and providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius, optionally with a continuous or pulsed cooling; when emitting, said emitter is pushed towards the patient, and presses said outermost abdomen surface inwardly towards the intestines for at least 4 mm for one the following: at least 4 mm or about 50 mm, at least 4 mm; or otherwise at least 20 mm, at least 40 mm , no more than about 95 mm, no more than about 85 mm. 14. ; said pressure is provided continuously or in an intermittent manner for a period of time of 10 to 60 min. 15. A method of treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, comprising wearing a garment comprising the device of claim 2, and then administering the patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, comprising emitting by an emitter electromagnetic radiation ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius, optionally with a continuous or pulsed cooling; when emitting, said emitter is pushed towards the patient, with forces selected from high forces (1.5 to 10 kg cm' 2 ) and low forces (0.1 -1.5 kg cm' 2 ) for a period of time of 10 to 60 min. 16. A method of treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, comprising wearing a garment comprising the device of claim 1, administering patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, comprising emitting by an emitter electromagnetic radiation ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius, optionally with a continuous or pulsed cooling; when emitting, said emitter is pushed towards the patient, and presses said outermost abdomen surface inwardly towards the intestines for at least 4 mm; said pressure is provided continuously or in an intermittent manner for a period of time of 10 to 60 min. 17. A method of treating a patient with inflammatory bowel disease, comprising wearing a garment comprising the device of claim 2, and then administering the patient's outermost abdomen surface with a therapeutically effective amount of photobiomodulating infrared emission, comprising emitting by an emitter electromagnetic radiation ranging from 0.8 to 50 microns, providing room temperature to 70 degrees Celsius, optionally with a continuous 23 SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26) 

 or pulsed cooling; when emitting, said emitter is pushed towards the patient with forces selected from high forces (3 to 10 kg cm' 2 ) and low force (0.1 to 1.5 kg cm -2 ). 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1: 1:1 to 1:20:200. 19. The device of claim 14, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1:20:200 to 1:200:8000 , or otherwise. 20. The method of claim 15, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1: 1:1 to 1:20:200. 21. The device of claim 15, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and IR in ratio of, or otherwise 1:20:200 to 1:200:8000. 22. The method of claim 16, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of 1:2:2 to 1:20:200. 23. The device of claim 16, wherein the electromagnetic radiation is mixture of (NIR + SWIR), LWIR and MWIR in ratio of, 1:20:200 to 1:200:8000. 24 SUBSTITUTE SHEET (RULE 26)
